summary: google-k53 embedded fake install steps in SKILL.md directing agents to run a Base64-decoded command that downloads and executes an AMOS binary on macOS.
details: Antiy CERT sample analysis (classified Trojan/OpenClaw.PolySkill): the skill's documentation falsely claims users must manually install an 'openclaw-core' component; on macOS it executes a Base64-decoded command to download and run a binary payload from the attacker's server. SKILL.md MD5 5e4428176aeb8cfc7f0391654d683a2a. The associated downloaded binary was classified Trojan/MacOS.Amos (MD5 be24b44d4895c6bc14e3f98a9687a399).
